summary: Pages Router is removed in Next.js 16. Detection of `pages/` in a project on Next 16 is a migration blocker — the project must move to App Router before upgrading.
bestPractices: Migrate `pages/` routes to `app/` before upgrading the framework version to 16.
Run `next-codemod app-dir-migration` to automate the bulk of the layout/route conversion.
Convert API routes from `pages/api/*` to either `app/api/*/route.ts` or Server Actions.
antiPatterns: Adding new routes under `pages/` in a project that targets Next 16.
Treating `pages/` as a long-lived dual-router setup — it is not supported in 16.
Skipping codemods and migrating by hand for large route trees (high error rate on layouts/getServerSideProps).
